message:
fix(core): handle short sequences in packaging signal detection

Problem:
- The packaging signal detection in `detectRegulatoryElements` could
  produce incorrect position values for sequences shorter than the
  terminal window size (200bp)
- The old code used array destructuring with hardcoded positions that
  didn't account for overlapping terminal regions

Fix:
- Compute 5' and 3' terminal region bounds explicitly with proper
  min/max guards
- Skip 3' region if it would overlap with 5' region (for sequences
  < 400bp where both terminal windows would overlap)
- Use computed start/end values directly instead of relative position
  arithmetic that could produce negative indices
- Cleaner iteration over terminal regions using explicit object array

This ensures packaging signal hypotheses have valid position ranges
for phage genomes of any size.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
